solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    bool checkInclusion(string s1, string s2) {
        if (s1.length() > s2.length()) return false;

        vector<int> countS1(26, 0);
        vector<int> countS2(26, 0);

        for (int i = 0; i < s1.length(); ++i) {
            countS1[s1[i] - 'a']++;
            countS2[s2[i] - 'a']++;
        }

        if (countS1 == countS2) return true;

        for (int i = s1.length(); i < s2.length(); ++i) {
            countS2[s2[i - s1.length()] - 'a']--;
            countS2[s2[i] - 'a']++;

            if (countS1 == countS2) return true;
        }

        return false;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n + m)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a sliding window approach with fixed window size equal to the length of s1. It maintains two count arrays for the characters in s1 and the current window in s2. By updating the count arrays as the window slides, it efficiently checks for a permutation match in constant time per step. The key strength is its linear time complexity and constant space usage.
